user.title: Connect to Wavin AHC 9000
user.description:
Enter the connection details for the AHC 9000 controller. The controller will be scanned automatically and all active channels will be set up with default names.

To rename channels or set thermostat types, open **Settings → Integrations → Wavin AHC 9000 → Configure** after setup.

data_description.host: IP address or hostname of the RS-485 bridge (e.g. 10.10.100.254).
data_description.port: TCP port on the USR-TCP232 gateway. Default is 8899.
data_description.slave_id: Modbus slave address of the AHC 9000. Usually 1.
data_description.scan_interval: How often to read register values from the controller (10–300 seconds).
data_description.num_thermostats: How many physical thermostats are connected to the AHC 9000. The scan is validated against this count — retry if the number of thermostats found does not match.

error.cannot_connect: Cannot connect to the device. Check the host, port, and that the bridge is reachable on the network.
error.unknown: Unexpected error — check the Home Assistant logs for details.
error.thermostat_count_mismatch: The scan found a different number of thermostats than expected. Wait a moment for all thermostats to communicate and try again.

init.title: General Settings
init.description: Adjust how often the controller is polled. Enable re-scan to detect any changes in the thermostat channel mapping.

data.rescan_channels: Re-scan device to update channel mapping

data_description.scan_interval: How often to read register values from the controller (10–300 seconds).
data_description.rescan_channels: Connect to the device and re-discover which channels belong to each thermostat. This overwrites the current mapping.
data_description.manual_groups: Skip the automatic scan and manually assign circuits to each thermostat. Ignored when Re-scan is also enabled.
data_description.num_thermostats: How many physical thermostats should the re-scan find. Also controls how many groups are shown in the manual configuration step.

groups.title: Manual Thermostat Groups
groups.description: Enter the circuit numbers (1–16, comma-separated) that belong to each thermostat. Each circuit must appear in exactly one group.

data_description.group_1_channels: Comma-separated circuit numbers that share this thermostat, e.g. 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6
data_description.group_2_channels: Comma-separated circuit numbers that share this thermostat, e.g. 7, 8, 9, 10, 11
data_description.group_3_channels: Comma-separated circuit numbers that share this thermostat, e.g. 13, 14, 15, 16
data_description.group_4_channels: Comma-separated circuit numbers that share this thermostat
data_description.group_5_channels: Comma-separated circuit numbers that share this thermostat
data_description.group_6_channels: Comma-separated circuit numbers that share this thermostat
data_description.group_7_channels: Comma-separated circuit numbers that share this thermostat
data_description.group_8_channels: Comma-separated circuit numbers that share this thermostat
data_description.group_9_channels: Comma-separated circuit numbers that share this thermostat
data_description.group_10_channels: Comma-separated circuit numbers that share this thermostat
data_description.group_11_channels: Comma-separated circuit numbers that share this thermostat
data_description.group_12_channels: Comma-separated circuit numbers that share this thermostat
data_description.group_13_channels: Comma-separated circuit numbers that share this thermostat
data_description.group_14_channels: Comma-separated circuit numbers that share this thermostat
data_description.group_15_channels: Comma-separated circuit numbers that share this thermostat
data_description.group_16_channels: Comma-separated circuit numbers that share this thermostat

zones.title: Channel Names & Thermostat Types

error.thermostat_count_mismatch: The scan found a different number of thermostats than expected. Wait a moment for all thermostats to communicate and try again.
error.empty_group: Each thermostat must have at least one circuit.
error.invalid_format: Enter circuit numbers separated by commas, e.g. 1, 2, 3.
error.channel_out_of_range: Circuit numbers must be between 1 and 16.
error.duplicate_channel: This circuit is already assigned to another thermostat.
